dialogueLines.speaker: Claire
dialogueLines.text: Left hand first. Surface area-wise, it makes sense to patch here and let it heal properly.

dialogueLines.speaker: Claire
dialogueLines.text: I use my right hand for everything. The patch will just fall off from too much movement. At least this way, one hand gets real rest.

dialogueLines.speaker: Stage
dialogueLines.text: [She carefully applies the antiseptic patch to her left hand. It's gentle, clean—a small moment of care. Then she reaches for the honey and garlic mixture.]

dialogueLines.speaker: Claire
dialogueLines.text: And the right hand… gets this.

dialogueLines.speaker: Stage
dialogueLines.text: [She dips her fingers into the mixture. The sting is immediate—sharp, biting, but oddly soothing beneath the pain. She hisses softly.]

dialogueLines.speaker: Claire
dialogueLines.text: Hnn—ngh. Okay. That's… that's the medicine working.

dialogueLines.speaker: Claire
dialogueLines.text: One hand heals gently. The other survives. I guess that's how it has to be.

dialogueLines.speaker: Narrator
dialogueLines.text: The applications done, Claire sits at the edge of her narrow bed. The room is small. The window dark. Outside, the world belongs to people who sleep.

dialogueLines.speaker: Claire
dialogueLines.text: I should rest now. Try to sleep before dawn. Before the day starts again.

dialogueLines.speaker: Stage
dialogueLines.text: [She lies down. The pillow is thin. The blanket has known better days. She closes her eyes.]

dialogueLines.speaker: Narrator
dialogueLines.text: But sleep doesn't come. Minutes pass. Then an hour. The darkness doesn't feel like rest—it feels like drowning.

dialogueLines.speaker: Claire
dialogueLines.text: I can't…

dialogueLines.speaker: Claire
dialogueLines.text: I don't know what will happen to me if I stay here another minute.

dialogueLines.speaker: Stage
dialogueLines.text: [She sits up. Her hands throb—one with gentle ache, one with the sharp reminder of survival. Before she can think better of it, she's on her feet.]

dialogueLines.speaker: Claire
dialogueLines.text: I need to go outside. Now.

dialogueLines.speaker: Narrator
dialogueLines.text: The streets at 3:45 AM are nearly empty. Lamplight pools on wet pavement—rain from earlier in the night. Claire walks without direction, just away, away from the house that has become her cage.

dialogueLines.speaker: Narrator
dialogueLines.text: At 4:10 AM, she sees it: a shophouse, windows softly glowing. Inside, through the glass, a man—sleeves rolled up—kneads tarts on the counter with practiced hands. And above the door: 'SUPPER WEEKEND til 5:00 AM'.

dialogueLines.speaker: Claire
dialogueLines.text: It's still open… and it looks…

dialogueLines.speaker: Narrator
dialogueLines.text: The café appears warm. Unthreatening. Safe. The man inside doesn't notice her yet.

dialogueLines.speaker: Claire
dialogueLines.text: It's warm. And I… I need warmth.

dialogueLines.speaker: Stage
dialogueLines.text: [With 35 minutes until closing, she takes a breath and walks in. The door chime is soft. The man looks up—and his expression shifts to something kind.]

dialogueLines.speaker: Kit (Wizard)
dialogueLines.text: Welcome. Come in, come in.

dialogueLines.speaker: Narrator
dialogueLines.text: Kit's eyes catch the state of her—wet clothes, trembling, cracked hands. His gaze lingers on the bandages and the stains of garlic still visible on her right hand.

dialogueLines.speaker: Kit (Wizard)
dialogueLines.text: What brings you here at this hour? And what's happened to your hands?

dialogueLines.speaker: Claire
dialogueLines.text: I… I saw the light. I needed somewhere to go.

dialogueLines.speaker: Stage
dialogueLines.text: [Kit listens quietly. Without asking more, he places a warm drink in front of her—not coffee, something richer, deeper. Then he disappears to the back.]

dialogueLines.speaker: Narrator
dialogueLines.text: He returns with ten small, perfectly formed tarts—each one golden, fragrant. And beside them: a bottle of iodine solution, the kind that truly heals.

dialogueLines.speaker: Kit (Wizard)
dialogueLines.text: This café is called the Wish House. You're welcome here. And these—

dialogueLines.speaker: Kit (Wizard)
dialogueLines.text: —are for someone who's been kind to their own hands. Garlic is strong. But the right healer knows you deserve better care now.

dialogueLines.speaker: Claire
dialogueLines.text: I don't… I don't understand.

dialogueLines.speaker: Kit (Wizard)
dialogueLines.text: You don't have to. Just rest. The world can wait.

cafeOrders.comments: Kit provides healing care—physical and emotional.

items.description: Iodine solution for proper wound healing
